solution.name: Conditional Access Policy Automation for Copilot

microsoftSourceClaims.claimText: The Microsoft Graph Office365 Conditional Access app-suite value lists Enterprise Copilot Platform (Microsoft 365 Copilot), and Microsoft recommends targeting the suite grouping instead of individual cloud apps to avoid service dependencies.

The Microsoft Graph conditionalAccessPolicy resource state property accepts the values enabled, disabled, and enabledForReportingButNotEnforced; this solution generates request bodies in report-only state by default.

Microsoft-managed and Baseline security mode Conditional Access policies are owned by Microsoft and appear with Microsoft in the Created by column; administrators can edit only their state and exclusions or duplicate them, so this scaffold does not attempt to own them.

Microsoft recommends excluding at least one emergency-access (break-glass) account from every Conditional Access policy so a misconfigured policy cannot lock every administrator out of the tenant; this solution models an excludeGroups slot and blocks live execution until it is populated.

Microsoft Entra Conditional Access named locations can represent IP ranges or countries and regions, and Microsoft Graph exposes read-only named-location inventory through identity/conditionalAccess/namedLocations with Policy.Read.All.

licenses: Microsoft Entra ID P1 for Conditional Access creation and enforcement
Microsoft Entra ID P2 only when risk-based Conditional Access signals are added to a later tenant design
Microsoft 365 Copilot entitlement assigned to a disposable lab user
roles: Security Reader for read-only Conditional Access, sign-in, and audit-log review
Microsoft Graph Policy.Read.All delegated permission for read-only policy and named-location inventory
features: Conditional Access enabled in the sanctioned disposable lab tenant
Sign-in and audit logs available for read-back evidence
capacityRequirements: One sanctioned disposable lab tenant with no production users
A designated disposable lab group reserved for future policy-targeting cycles
dataRequirements: Representative sample configuration and templates only
No production Conditional Access policies are created, modified, or deleted in this cycle
prerequisites.notes: READ-ONLY FIRST CYCLE. mutations is intentionally empty and the live mutation is deferred. The intended later mutation is to create a single narrowly scoped, disposable, report-only (state=enabledForReportingButNotEnforced) Conditional Access policy and then delete it. It is deferred until the executing adapter can structurally enforce every one of these preconditions: independent positive tenant-ID proof captured before any write; a designated disposable user or group as the only include target; explicit exclusion of emergency-access and automation identities; a unique run-ownership marker in the policy displayName; no tenant-wide (All users) targeting; no enabled enforcement; post-create read-back; bounded propagation polling; cleanup limited to the current run-owned object; deletion read-back; and fail-on-orphan. Because this template-bound FSI contract schema cannot encode those guarantees as enforceable fields, this cycle stays read-only and never executes a live mutation.

phases.title: Confirm preconditions and independent tenant proof

steps.intent: Capture positive tenant identity from Microsoft Graph and compare it with the sanctioned tenant record before any Conditional Access inspection.
steps.expected: The organization id and displayName returned by Microsoft Graph match the separately maintained sanctioned disposable-lab tenant record.
steps.operatorNote: Compare the Graph organization response with the out-of-band lab authorization record. Do not proceed if either the directory identifier or organization name differs.

readBack.reference: Microsoft Graph organization response and out-of-band lab authorization record
readBack.expectation: Graph tenant identity matches the separately maintained sanctioned lab record.

steps.intent: Confirm Entra ID P1 licensing and the Security Reader access required for this cycle, and classify P2 only if later risk-based signals are in scope.
steps.expected: P1 and Security Reader are present; P2 is recorded as optional unless risk-based policy conditions are explicitly in scope.
steps.locator: Microsoft Entra admin center > Identity > Billing/Licenses and Roles & administrators

readBack.expectation: P1 and Security Reader are confirmed; P2 applicability is recorded without blocking this non-risk-based cycle.

steps.intent: Review existing Conditional Access policies that target the Office365 app suite and identify which are Microsoft-managed versus solution-owned.
steps.expected: Policies targeting the Office365 app suite are visible, and the Created by column distinguishes Microsoft-managed policies that this solution must not own.
steps.locator: Microsoft Entra admin center > Protection > Conditional Access > Policies

readBack.expectation: Operator confirms the policy list and the Created by column without changing any policy.

steps.intent: Read the named-location inventory needed to assess recommended and regulated policy templates without exposing configured IP ranges in contract evidence.
steps.expected: The response identifies configured named locations and metadata needed for policy review; no named location is created, modified, or deleted.
steps.operatorNote: Retain only id, displayName, type, timestamps, trust flag, and country metadata in evidence. Do not retain configured IP ranges.
steps.command: $response = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identity/conditionalAccess/namedLocations'; $response.value | ForEach-Object { [pscustomobject]@{ id = $_.id; displayName = $_.displayName; type = $_.'@odata.type'; createdDateTime = $_.createdDateTime; modifiedDateTime = $_.modifiedDateTime; isTrusted = $_.isTrusted; countryLookupMethod = $_.countryLookupMethod; includeUnknownCountriesAndRegions = $_.includeUnknownCountriesAndRegions } } | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5

readBack.expectation: Named-location metadata is available for review and contains no retained IP-range values.

steps.intent: Run the compliance monitor in documentation-first mode to emit representative drift and compliance artifacts.
steps.expected: Representative compliance-status.json and drift-alert-summary.json are produced from sample data without connecting to the tenant.

readBack.expectation: Output includes representative compliance and drift findings with documentation-first markers.

steps.intent: Generate report-only policy templates and confirm the break-glass excludeGroups slot and requiresBreakGlassExclusion flag are present.
steps.expected: ca-policy-templates.json shows report-only request bodies with an excludeGroups slot, and policies are flagged requiresBreakGlassExclusion when no exclusion group is configured.

readBack.expectation: Templates are report-only, include the excludeGroups slot, and the Graph command header warns about excluding emergency-access accounts.

phases.title: Verify Microsoft source currency and read-back

steps.intent: Confirm the Microsoft Learn sources recorded in microsoftSourceClaims remain published and match their recorded lifecycle state.
steps.expected: Each cited Microsoft Learn source resolves and still supports its claim, including the Office365 app-suite contents and Microsoft-managed policy ownership.
steps.operatorNote: Open each microsoftSourceClaims sourceUrl and confirm the page still supports the claim and lifecycle state; record any drift as a BLOCKED disposition with source evidence.

readBack.expectation: All cited sources remain published and consistent with the recorded lifecycle state.

steps.intent: Perform a read-only Microsoft Graph query to confirm Conditional Access policy read access and that returned state values match the documented enum.
steps.expected: The read-only request returns policies whose state values fall within enabled, disabled, or enabledForReportingButNotEnforced.

readBack.expectation: Response is read-only and state values match the documented enum; no policy is created or changed.

steps.intent: Capture operator attestation that no Conditional Access object was created, modified, or deleted during this read-only cycle.
steps.expected: The operator confirms no tenant mutation occurred, so no cleanup is required for this cycle.
steps.operatorNote: Attest that this cycle was read-only: no Conditional Access policy, named location, or group was created, modified, or deleted. The live mutation remains deferred per prerequisites.notes.

readBack.expectation: Attestation confirms no mutable change occurred and cleanup is not required.

requiredArtifacts.description: Independent positive tenant-identity proof captured before inspection.
Read-only Monitor-Compliance and Deploy-Solution output showing representative drift, compliance, and break-glass slot findings.
Entra admin center Conditional Access policy list showing the Created by column and Office365 app-suite targeting.
Read-only named-location metadata inventory with configured IP ranges omitted from retained evidence.
Microsoft Learn source citations confirming app-suite contents, policy state enum, managed-policy ownership, and emergency-access exclusion guidance.
